globalDisclaimer: This is not a diagnosis. It describes tendencies from your answers today. Do not use these results for hiring, school admission, or labeling other people.
confidenceNote: These traits are sensitive. If every score looks very high or very low, try again when you can answer honestly. Scores can change with mood and context.

low.summary: You rarely use strategy or deception to get what you want. You prefer direct, fair approaches.
strengths: People often trust your motives
You build goodwill instead of burning bridges
You avoid games that backfire later
blindSpots: You may miss when others are playing politics
You may give away leverage in competitive settings
low.contextCaveat: Low scores here do not mean you are naive — you may simply dislike manipulation.

typical.summary: You can be strategic when needed, but it is not your default mode.
strengths: You read situations before you act
You can negotiate without going cold
You balance honesty with practicality
blindSpots: Under pressure you may slip into sharper tactics than you like
Others may not know which version of you they are getting
typical.contextCaveat: Context matters: work politics and personal life pull different behavior.

high.summary: You often plan ahead, use charm or strategy, and focus on your own goals in social situations.
strengths: You can navigate politics and power
You protect your interests in tough rooms
You see angles others miss
blindSpots: People may feel used even when you think you were fair
Trust can erode if strategy becomes a habit
high.contextCaveat: High scores describe tendency, not a fixed character label.

low.summary: You usually care about rules and other people's feelings. Guilt and empathy show up for you.
strengths: You repair harm when you cause it
You take morality seriously in decisions
You notice when someone is hurt
blindSpots: You may struggle in cutthroat environments that reward coldness
You may judge harshly when others seem uncaring
low.contextCaveat: This scale measures subclinical traits, not a personality disorder.

typical.summary: You can be cool-headed, but you are not usually cold or reckless.
strengths: You stay calm under stress
You can make hard calls without panicking
You still feel remorse when you cross a line
blindSpots: Sometimes you under-react to others' pain
Sometimes you over-react and feel guilty later
typical.contextCaveat: Sleep, stress, and anger can temporarily shift this score.

high.summary: You may show low remorse, low concern for rules, and a cooler emotional style in conflicts.
strengths: You stay steady in crises
You can act fast without getting stuck in guilt
You are hard to rattle
blindSpots: Others may see you as harsh or uncaring
Risk-taking can go too far when bored or challenged
high.contextCaveat: A high score is not the same as being a bad person or having a clinical condition.

low.summary: You do not strongly need admiration, status, or special treatment from others.
strengths: You share credit easily
You can listen without steering attention back to you
You feel okay when you are not the center
blindSpots: You may tolerate less recognition than you deserve
low.contextCaveat: Low narcissism is not the same as low confidence.

typical.summary: You want respect and recognition sometimes, but it does not run your life.
strengths: You can advocate for yourself when it matters
You enjoy praise without needing it constantly
You balance ego with empathy
blindSpots: Competition can bring out a sharper side
Rejection may sting more than you admit
typical.contextCaveat: Social media and work culture can temporarily push this score up.

high.summary: You often want admiration, status, and special treatment. Being overlooked can bother you.
strengths: You project confidence
You pursue goals others avoid
You can inspire or lead when self-focus is managed
blindSpots: Relationships may strain if your needs always come first
Feedback can feel like an attack
high.contextCaveat: High scores point to patterns to watch, not a lifetime label.
